enonce.texte:
Look at the email below and answer the questions.

📧 Email

To: teacher@school.com

From: amina@gmail.com

Subject: Homework question

Message: Dear Teacher, I have a question about the homework. Can you explain exercise 5? Thank you. Best regards, Amina

questions.reponse: The email is to the teacher.

questions.texte: What is the subject of the email?
What does Amina want to know?
questions.reponse: She wants to know about exercise 5.

enonce.texte:
Complete the email with the missing words from the box.

Words: To, From, Subject, Message, Dear, Best regards

______: friend@yahoo.com

______: mike@gmail.com

______: Invitation to my party

______: ______ John, I am having a party on Saturday. Can you come? Please reply soon. ______, Mike

questions.texte: Complete the email with the correct words.
questions.reponse: To: friend@yahoo.com; From: mike@gmail.com; Subject: Invitation to my party; Message: Dear John, I am having a party on Saturday. Can you come? Please reply soon. Best regards, Mike

sousQuestions.texte: Write the missing words in the correct places.
sousQuestions.reponse: To, From, Subject, Message, Dear, Best regards

enonce.texte: Write a short email (2–3 sentences) to a friend. Invite him/her to your birthday party. Include: To, From, Subject, and Message.

questions.texte: Write a short email to a friend.
questions.reponse: To: friend@gmail.com; From: me@gmail.com; Subject: Birthday party; Message: Hello, I'm having a birthday party on Saturday. Can you come? Please let me know.

sousQuestions.texte: Write the email with all parts.

enonce.texte:
Rearrange the jumbled parts to form a correct email.

Jumbled parts:

  • Message: I have a question about the test.
  • From: student@school.com
  • Subject: Question about test
  • Best regards, Ali
  • To: teacher@school.com
  • Dear Teacher,

questions.texte: Rearrange the parts to form a complete email.
questions.reponse: To: teacher@school.com; From: student@school.com; Subject: Question about test; Message: Dear Teacher, I have a question about the test. Best regards, Ali

sousQuestions.texte: Put the parts in the correct order.
sousQuestions.reponse: To, From, Subject, Message, Dear Teacher, (body), Best regards, Ali

intitule: Writing a Formal Email to a Teacher

enonce.texte: Write a short formal email (3–4 sentences) to your teacher asking about the time of an exam. Use polite language.

questions.texte: Write a formal email to your teacher.
questions.reponse: To: teacher@school.com; From: myname@gmail.com; Subject: Exam time; Message: Dear Teacher, Could you please tell me the time of the English exam? I want to make sure I am on time. Thank you. Best regards, [Your Name]

sousQuestions.texte: Write a polite email with all parts.

intitule: Writing an Email to a Pen Pal

enonce.texte: Write an email (4–5 sentences) to a pen pal. Introduce yourself, talk about your school and favourite subject, and ask a question about him/her.

questions.texte: Write a complete email to a pen pal.
questions.reponse: To: penpal@email.com; From: myname@gmail.com; Subject: Hello from Cameroon; Message: Dear Pen Pal, My name is [Your Name]. I am 12 years old and I live in Cameroon. I study in Class 6. My favourite subject is English. I like reading and playing football. What about you? What is your favourite subject? Please write back soon. Best regards, [Your Name]
